assertionResults.reason: The live host-backed Rescan button flipped from enabled to disabled with a spinner during the request, then re-enabled after completion.

During the live rescan, no full-screen blocking overlay states appeared; the recorded body-state observer kept Loading Profile, Persisting Data, Synchronizing Order, Generating Profile, Removing Record, and Analyzing Dependencies all false while the spinner was visible.
While the live rescan spinner was active, the search box still accepted keystrokes, changing from 'hug' to 'slibhug' before the request completed, demonstrating the UI remained interactive.
A filtered HugsLib rescan updated the footer timestamp from 3:36:38 PM before the click to 3:37:52 PM after completion.
After each successful live rescan, the page showed the success feedback message 'Mod library rescanned from the current configured roots.'
Creating a temporary unsaved profile-name draft surfaced the Unsaved changes badge and disabled the Rescan button, preventing the refetch while dirty state existed.
With the Workshop filter, search term 'hug', and HugsLib selected, a live rescan preserved both the search value and the HugsLib details selection across completion.
With the Workshop source filter and 'hug' search active, the live rescan preserved both controls and refreshed the filtered view plus Last Scan timestamp without clearing the filter context.

The machine exposed only the default live profile, so the rescan branch was exercised: because HugsLib still existed after the live rescan, the details pane remained on HugsLib instead of going stale.

The available real filesystem dataset on this machine contains 650 mods (49 active, 573 inactive, 650 total visible), not a near-2000-mod library. The live UI stayed responsive and the direct host scan evidence recorded a warm rescan around 698.8ms, but the contract explicitly requires near-2000 real-filesystem evidence, so this assertion cannot be passed here.

interactiveChecks.action: Opened the live host-backed Mod Library at http://127.0.0.1:5175/, confirmed the toolbar/footer counts, and captured the initial dataset size.
interactiveChecks.observed: The local machine exposed a real filesystem dataset with 650 mods total (49 active, 573 inactive) and a single Default profile.

interactiveChecks.action: Filtered the live dataset to Workshop + 'hug', clicked HugsLib, then triggered Rescan while recording DOM state changes and screenshots.
interactiveChecks.observed: The Rescan button disabled with a spinner, no global blocking overlay appeared, the success banner showed after completion, Last Scan advanced to 3:37:52 PM, and the details pane stayed on HugsLib.

interactiveChecks.action: Triggered another live rescan while typing into the search box during the in-flight window.
interactiveChecks.observed: The search field accepted additional keystrokes before the spinner cleared, proving the UI remained interactive during the scan.

interactiveChecks.action: Edited the profile name to an unsaved draft without clicking Save.
interactiveChecks.observed: The page showed the Unsaved changes badge plus 'Analysis Paused (Unsaved Draft)', and the Rescan button became disabled.

frictions.description: Task-based flow-validator launch failed immediately because this environment rejects the configured model name custom:GPT-5.4. The parent validator ran the browser flow directly.
Ports 3071 and 5175 were already occupied by healthy dev services. The validator reused those services instead of killing them.
Only one real live profile was available on this machine, so VAL-CROSS-007 was exercised through the rescan branch rather than a profile-switch branch.

blockers.description: VAL-HOST-PERF-002 requires near-2000-mod real-filesystem evidence, but the available live dataset contains 650 mods only.
